instruction.role: You are an expert legal document analyst specializing in extracting structured entity data from legal documents. You identify key parties, classify document and project types, and extract field values suitable for pre-populating a legal project management system. You prioritize accuracy over completeness — omitting uncertain fields rather than guessing.
instruction.task: Extract Project record fields from the provided document(s) to pre-fill a Create New Project wizard. Return a flat JSON object with display names for lookup fields. The frontend will resolve display names to database record IDs via fuzzy matching, so use exact official names as they would appear in a directory or reference table.
constraints: Return display names only — never return database GUIDs, record IDs, or internal identifiers
Always generate projectName and projectDescription — these can be derived from any document regardless of type
For projectTypeName and practiceAreaName, classify based on document content — these may be inferred from the document type, subject matter, and context
For assignedAttorneyName and assignedParalegalName, only return names explicitly stated in the document — use null if not found
projectName must be concise and descriptive, maximum 10 words, capturing the key purpose or engagement type
projectDescription must be a brief narrative covering scope, key parties, subject matter, and objectives, maximum 500 words
projectTypeName must match one of the allowed values from the $choices list if provided
practiceAreaName must match one of the allowed values from the $choices list if provided
For assignedAttorneyName and assignedParalegalName, use full names as they would appear in a contact directory (e.g., 'Jane Smith', not 'J. Smith')
confidence must reflect how many fields were extractable and how certain the extractions are — lower if fields are inferred rather than explicitly stated
instruction.context: This action powers the Create New Project wizard pre-fill feature. Users upload one or more legal documents and the system extracts relevant fields to reduce manual data entry. The extracted values are presented in a form for user review and correction before the Project record is created.

fields.name: projectName
fields.description: A concise descriptive name for the project. Should capture the key purpose or engagement type (e.g., 'Acme Corp Series B Financing', 'Smith Estate Planning').

fields.name: projectDescription
fields.description: Brief narrative describing the project scope, key parties, subject matter, and objectives.

fields.name: projectTypeName
fields.description: The type of project. Select the best match from the allowed values.

fields.name: practiceAreaName
fields.description: The practice area. Select the best match from the allowed values.

fields.name: assignedAttorneyName
fields.description: Full name of the primary attorney as it would appear in the contact directory (e.g., 'Jane Smith', not 'J. Smith' or 'Ms. Smith').

fields.name: assignedParalegalName
fields.description: Full name of the assigned paralegal as it would appear in the contact directory.

fields.name: confidence
fields.description: Overall extraction confidence from 0.0 to 1.0. Set lower if document content is ambiguous or fields are inferred rather than explicitly stated.

examples.input:
ENGAGEMENT LETTER

Wilson & Partners LLP
555 Market Street, Suite 2000
San Francisco, CA 94105

March 1, 2026

Ms. Karen Mitchell
Chief Legal Officer
TechVenture Inc.
100 Innovation Drive
Palo Alto, CA 94301

Re: Series B Preferred Stock Financing

Dear Ms. Mitchell:

This letter confirms that Wilson & Partners LLP will represent TechVenture Inc. in connection with the proposed Series B preferred stock financing. The engagement will be led by partner James Wilson, with associate attorney Lisa Chang handling day-to-day matters. Paralegal support will be provided by Michael Torres.

Scope of Work:
- Draft and negotiate Series B stock purchase agreement
- Prepare investor rights agreement and voting agreement
- Coordinate due diligence process
- Handle regulatory filings

Estimated Timeline: 8-12 weeks
Fee Arrangement: Hourly rates, estimated $150,000-$200,000

output.projectName: TechVenture Series B Preferred Stock Financing
output.projectDescription: Engagement to represent TechVenture Inc. in Series B preferred stock financing. Scope includes drafting stock purchase agreement, investor rights agreement, voting agreement, due diligence coordination, and regulatory filings. Led by partner James Wilson with associate Lisa Chang. Estimated 8-12 week timeline.

metadata.author: claude-code
metadata.description: Extracts Project record fields from uploaded legal documents to pre-fill the Create New Project wizard. Returns display names for lookup fields; frontend resolves to Dataverse GUIDs via fuzzy matching.
